title: Asset sourcing

prerequisites.description: Define shared request dataclasses (MusicRequest, BrollRequest, VoiceoverRequest, AssetRequest) in autopilot/source/__init__.py with fields matching EDL tool-use schemas, plus a resolve_status field for tracking resolution state

steps.description: Write tests for source __init__.py: request dataclass imports, field validation (MusicRequest has mood/duration/start_time/resolved_path, BrollRequest has description/duration/start_time/resolved_path, VoiceoverRequest has text/start_time/duration/resolved_path, AssetRequest is a union type alias), and __all__ exports

Implement request dataclasses in autopilot/source/__init__.py: MusicRequest, BrollRequest, VoiceoverRequest dataclasses with fields from EDL schemas + resolved_path: Path | None. Define AssetRequest as Union type. Export in __all__

Write tests for voiceover.py: test VoiceoverError importable, test generate_voiceover signature (text, output_path, config), test kokoro engine path with mocked kokoro module (patches sys.modules['kokoro']), test elevenlabs engine path with mocked requests/httpx, test output .wav file written to output_path, test unknown engine raises VoiceoverError

Implement autopilot/source/voiceover.py: generate_voiceover(text, output_path, config) dispatches to _generate_kokoro() or _generate_elevenlabs() based on config.tts_engine. Kokoro path loads model and generates wav. ElevenLabs path calls API. Returns path to generated .wav. Raises VoiceoverError on failure

Write tests for music.py: test MusicError importable, test source_music signature (request, config, output_dir), test musicgen engine path with mocked audiocraft module (MusicGen.get_pretrained, model.generate, torchaudio.save), test freesound search with mocked requests (successful + empty results), test fetch_list_only config skips generation and returns None, test returns Path to generated/downloaded audio file, test API key from env var

Implement autopilot/source/music.py: source_music(request, config, output_dir) with _generate_musicgen() for local generation and _search_freesound() for API search. MusicGen path uses audiocraft.models.MusicGen. Freesound path uses requests + FREESOUND_API_KEY env var. Returns Path | None

Write tests for broll.py: test BrollError importable, test source_broll signature (request, output_dir), test Pexels API search with mocked requests (returns video results, downloads top-3), test Pixabay fallback when Pexels returns no results, test returns Path to downloaded video, test API keys from env vars (PEXELS_API_KEY, PIXABAY_API_KEY), test returns None when both APIs fail, test rate limit handling

Implement autopilot/source/broll.py: source_broll(request, output_dir) with _search_pexels() primary and _search_pixabay() fallback. Downloads top-3 results from Pexels, falls back to Pixabay. Uses PEXELS_API_KEY and PIXABAY_API_KEY env vars. Returns Path | None

Write tests for fetch_list.py: test generate_fetch_list signature (unresolved, output_path), test writes valid markdown file, test format includes type/description/suggested_sources/priority columns, test handles empty list (writes header-only markdown), test handles mix of MusicRequest/BrollRequest/VoiceoverRequest, test file written to specified output_path

Implement autopilot/source/fetch_list.py: generate_fetch_list(unresolved, output_path) writes a human-readable markdown table listing each unresolved AssetRequest with type, description, suggested sources (per type), and priority. Handles all three request types with appropriate suggested source recommendations

Write tests for resolve.py: test resolve_edl_assets signature (edl, config, output_dir, db), test extracts MusicRequests from edl['music'], BrollRequests from edl['broll_requests'], VoiceoverRequests from edl['voiceovers'], test resolved assets get file paths inserted into EDL entries, test unresolved assets collected into fetch_list.md, test EDL stored back to DB with resolved paths, test full pipeline integration with mocked source modules

Implement autopilot/source/resolve.py: resolve_edl_assets(edl, config, output_dir, db) extracts requests from EDL arrays, calls source_music/source_broll/generate_voiceover for each, updates EDL entries with resolved_path field, collects unresolved requests, calls generate_fetch_list for unresolved, stores updated EDL in DB via db.upsert_edit_plan, and updates narrative status to 'sourced'

design_decisions.decision: Define request dataclasses in autopilot/source/__init__.py rather than a separate types.py
design_decisions.rationale: The request types are small, shared across the source subpackage, and following the project convention of keeping related types near their usage (like config.py has all config dataclasses). The __init__.py already exists empty and is the natural place for shared types within this subpackage.

design_decisions.decision: Create a separate resolve.py orchestrator module instead of putting orchestration in __init__.py
design_decisions.rationale: The resolution logic (extract from EDL → source → update EDL → write fetch_list → persist) is substantial enough to warrant its own module. This follows the separation of concerns pattern seen elsewhere (e.g., edl.py orchestrates LLM calls + validation + persistence). It also makes it independently testable.

design_decisions.decision: Use environment variables for API keys (FREESOUND_API_KEY, PEXELS_API_KEY, PIXABAY_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY) rather than config.yaml
design_decisions.rationale: API keys are secrets that shouldn't be in config files (which are checked into git). Environment variables are the standard approach and align with the existing pattern (anthropic SDK reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env). This also makes testing easier — just mock os.environ.

design_decisions.decision: AssetRequest as Union type alias rather than base class inheritance
design_decisions.rationale: Python Union type (MusicRequest | BrollRequest | VoiceoverRequest) is simpler, more Pythonic for this use case, and avoids unnecessary class hierarchy. The fetch_list.py can use isinstance() checks to determine request type for formatting.

design_decisions.decision: Add resolved_path field to EDL dict entries rather than creating separate resolution mapping
design_decisions.rationale: Mutating the EDL in-place (adding resolved_path to music/voiceover/broll_request entries) keeps the data co-located and is the simplest approach. The PRD states 'Updated EDL with resolved file paths replacing placeholder requests' which implies in-place modification. The updated EDL is then stored back via db.upsert_edit_plan.

design_decisions.decision: Mock all external dependencies at the sys.modules level in tests
design_decisions.rationale: Following the established pattern from test_edl.py, test_asr.py, and test_audio_events.py which mock anthropic, whisperx, panns_inference etc. at sys.modules level. This avoids requiring ML model downloads or API credentials in the test environment.

design_decisions.decision: source_broll downloads top-3 results to disk, returns list of Paths, letting caller select
design_decisions.rationale: The PRD specifies 'Download top-3 results. Let calling code select best match.' The resolve.py orchestrator can then select the first result or implement a selection heuristic. This avoids coupling download logic with selection logic.

reuse.what: ModelConfig dataclass with tts_engine and music_engine fields
reuse.how: Import ModelConfig as the config parameter for source_music() and generate_voiceover(). The engine selection fields (tts_engine: kokoro|elevenlabs, music_engine: musicgen|fetch_list_only) are already defined with validation.

resolve.py uses db.upsert_edit_plan() to store the updated EDL with resolved asset paths, and db.get_edit_plan() to load the EDL for resolution. Also uses db.update_narrative_status() to mark narratives as 'sourced'.
Use the existing catalog_db fixture for integration tests in test_resolve.py that need to seed edit plans and verify DB updates.

reuse.what: sys.modules patching pattern for mocking heavy dependencies
reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: create MagicMock modules, patch sys.modules, then import the module under test. Used for audiocraft (MusicGen), kokoro, and API client mocking.

resolve.py reads these arrays to extract asset requests. The fields map directly to the request dataclasses: music[].mood → MusicRequest.mood, broll_requests[].description → BrollRequest.description, voiceovers[].text → VoiceoverRequest.text.

reuse.where: All autopilot modules (edl.py, script.py, audio_events.py, etc.)
reuse.how: Each new module uses logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) for consistent log output.

reuse.what: Exception class pattern (custom Error subclass per module)
reuse.how: Define MusicError, BrollError, VoiceoverError following the same pattern: simple Exception subclass with docstring, listed in __all__.
